relatedness_reasoning: The inbound file documents a lending decision override event (point-in-time approval), while the outbound file contains monthly repayment instructions (recurring operational transactions). Although both involve loans and customers, they serve fundamentally different purposes in different business processes with no clear data flow connection. The inbound file provides approval metadata and risk assessment; the outbound file requires repayment scheduling, bank account details, and direct debit mandates that are not present in the inbound schema.
